2604.12421.title: Agentic Insight Generation in VSM Simulations
2604.12421.paper_summary: Extracting actionable insights from complex value stream map simulations can be challenging, time-consuming, and error-prone. Recent advances in large language models offer new avenues to support users with this task. While existing approaches excel at processing raw data to gain information, they are structurally unfit to pick up on subtle situational differences needed to distinguish similar data sources in this domain. To address this issue, we propose a decoupled, two-step agentic architecture. By separating orchestration from data analysis, the system leverages progressive data discovery infused with domain expert knowledge. This architecture allows the orchestration to intelligently select data sources and perform multi-hop reasoning across data structures while maintaining a slim internal context. Results from multiple state-of-the-art large language models demonstrate the framework's viability: with top-tier models achieving accuracies of up to 86% and demonstrating high robustness across evaluation runs.
2604.12421.paper_summary_zh: 從複雜的價值流圖模擬中提取可行的見解可能具有挑戰性、耗時且容易出錯。最近在大型語言模型方面的進展為支持用戶完成這項任務提供了新的途徑。雖然現有的方法在處理原始數據以獲取信息方面表現出色，但它們在結構上不適合捕捉微妙的情境差異，這些差異在此領域中區分相似數據來源是必要的。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種解耦的兩步驟代理架構。通過將編排與數據分析分開，系統利用了注入領域專家知識的漸進式數據發現。這種架構使編排能夠智能地選擇數據來源並在數據結構之間進行多跳推理，同時保持精簡的內部上下文。來自多個最先進的大型語言模型的結果證明了該框架的可行性：頂級模型的準確率高達86%，並在評估運行中表現出高穩健性。
2604.12421.author: Micha Selak et.al.
2604.12421.authors: Micha Selak, Dirk Krechel, Adrian Ulges, Sven Spieckermann, Niklas Stoehr, Andreas Loehr
